items.title: Gold falls as renewed Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Monday as renewed US-Iran tensions pushed the dollar and oil prices higher, fuelling fears of inflation and reinforcing the higher-for-longer interest rate outlook. Spot gold was down 0.8 per cent at US$4,498.89 per ounce at 0909 GMT after hitting a two-week high on Friday. US gold futures for August delivery fell 1.4 per cent to US$4,528.90.

items.title: Five hurt in five‑vehicle collision in Kudat
items.summary: KUDAT, June 1 — Five people were injured in a road accident involving five vehicles at Jalan Kampung Kandawayon here late yesterday. Kudat Fire and Rescue Station chief Darikson Galanang said the crash involved a Perodua Axia, a Proton, a Toyota Revo and two Perodua Myvi cars. Two other injured victims, aged 24 and 26, were the driver and passenger of a grey Myvi.

items.title: Johor state assembly dissolved, Onn Hafiz cites need for mandate to drive development
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — The Johor state legislative assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the state’s upcoming election. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution after obtaining the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim earlier today. He said the move would allow the people of Johor to elect a new state government and secure a mandate to continue the state’s development agenda.

items.title: Man missing, feared drowned after boat overturns near SK Nanga Balang in Kapit
items.summary: KANOWIT, June 1 — A 32-year-old man is feared drowned after a boat carrying seven passengers capsized near SK Nanga Balang in Gaat, Kapit, this morning. The victim, identified as Spielberg Dylan Sekunyir, was reported missing following the incident, while six other passengers managed to reach safety. A spokesperson for the department said firefighters from the Kapit Fire and Rescue Station (BBP Kapit) were dispatched to the scene after first proceeding to the Kapit police station to obtain information on the incident.

items.title: China accuses Japan defence minister of 'baseless' claims
items.summary: BEIJING: China accused Japan's defence minister on Monday of spreading "baseless" claims and sowing confusion, a day after he took a veiled swipe at Beijing. Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi had pledged on Sunday to keep strengthening Japan's military and warned China was expanding its capabilities without sufficient transparency. China's foreign ministry said on Monday that Koizumi's comments were "entirely baseless."

items.title: Hamzah allies back Tuan Ibrahim's push for Muafakat Nasional revival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two allies of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, both of whom were sacked from Bersatu, have backed Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man's proposal to revive Muafakat Nasional (MN). Former Bersatu supreme council member Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah welcomed the proposal, saying it was consistent with Hamzah's vision of building a grand political coalition ahead of the 16th General Election (GE16). The Indera Mahkota member of Parliament said Hamzah had unveiled the "New National Consensus" framework on June 22 last year, aimed at making Malaysia a more advanced, prosperous and harmonious nation.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolves, election within 60 days
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor state assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the next state election to be held within 60 days. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia and the Johor Regent, had consented to the dissolution of the assembly. Pakatan Harapan holds 12 seats, Perikatan Nasional three, and Muda one.

items.title: Auto dealers expect new hire-purchase rules to boost buyer interest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The implementation of the reducing balance method and effective interest rate system under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 is expected to boost business, say second-hand car dealers and potential buyers. Car salesman Aviss Thung said the new method, where interest is calculated based on the outstanding principal instead of the original loan amount, would make financing easier to explain to customers. But once they return and understand the new rules, I expect more interest in car purchases," he said.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang and Tengku Ampuan extend RMAF Day greetings
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, and the Tengku Ampuan of Pahang, Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah, have extended their warmest greetings to all Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) personnel on the occasion of RMAF Day 2026. In a post on the Kesultanan Pahang 's Facebook page today, Their Royal Highnesses conveyed their deepest appreciation for the sacrifices, dedication and unwavering commitment shown by RMAF in defending the nation's airspace and safeguarding the Malaysian people. "Happy Royal Malaysian Air Force Day 2026.

items.title: Public rescue injured driver, passenger after crash near SMK Sematan, says Sarawak Fire Dept
items.summary: KUCHING, June 1 — A man was seriously injured in a road accident involving a sedan in front of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sematan yesterday afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) Operations Centre said the injured man was the driver of the vehicle involved in the accident. The male driver was not trapped but sustained serious injuries, while a female passenger also suffered injuries.

items.title: Philippines, Vietnam explore more direct flights, sign tourism deal
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippines and Vietnam have agreed to strengthen tourism cooperation and improve air connectivity following the signing of a Tourism Cooperation Programme covering 2026 to 2029, Philippine News Agency (PNA) reported. President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. said both countries would work to increase flight frequencies and facilitate greater tourism exchanges. "Building upon a strong performance in 2025, which saw nearly half a million Filipino travellers going to Vietnam and a rising number of Vietnamese tourists coming to our shores, we have agreed to work closely to enhance air connectivity and increase commercial flight frequencies," Marcos said.

items.title: Marc Ratings forecasts ringgit to range between 3.92-4.07 vs US dollar in short term
items.summary: The firm said that the revision was primarily externally driven, as market expectations of two rate cuts by the US Federal Reserve (Fed) have been priced out following the Middle East war. In its monthly review report, the firm said that Malaysia's growth remains well supported by both domestic and external demand, providing a strong buffer against near-term uncertainties. Additionally, it said the surge in external demand reflects ongoing supply chain diversification and geopolitical realignment, strengthening Malaysia's position as a trade-oriented economy.

items.title: Family among four killed in Toyota Vios as BMW, Mercedes, Wira and Alza collide near Simpang Renggam Prison
items.summary: KLUANG, June 1 — Four people were killed and two others injured in a collision involving five vehicles along Jalan Renggam–Simpang Renggam, in front of Simpang Renggam Prison, this afternoon. The crash, which occurred at about 1pm, involved a Perodua Alza, Proton Wira, Mercedes Benz, BMW and Toyota Vios, Harian Metro reported. Four occupants of the Mercedes Benz also survived: Mohd Azhar Ahmad, 63; his son Muhammad Hariz Mohd Azhar, 23; Rahimar A Rahman, 57; and a female passenger whose identity is still unknown.

items.title: Rescuers find new access route in search for two missing Laos miners
items.summary: BANGKOK: Rescuers searching for two miners missing in a flooded cave system in Laos said they had discovered a previously unknown entrance that could provide access to the area where the men are believed to be trapped. Australian cave diver Josh Richards told dpa that he, French cave expert Robin Cuesta and soldiers found the opening after trekking through dense forest in the mountainous Xaisomboun province. Richards said the newly discovered access route could provide a safer alternative.

items.title: 'Waste of time': Puad on calls to revive Muafakat Nasional
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An Umno supreme council member has dismissed calls to revive Muafakat Nasional (MN), describing the proposal as a "waste of time". Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi said he was confident Umno was no longer interested in MN and would not support the proposal for an MN 2.0 raised by Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man. "As an Umno supreme council member, I am confident that Umno is no longer interested in MN.

items.title: Negri Sembilan RTD issues 3,062 notices during special Aidiladha op
items.summary: State RTD director Jasyindar Singh Sihdu said 1,673 of the vehicles inspected were taken action against for various offences under the Road Transport Act 1987. He said the highest number of offences recorded was 1,055 involving major violations such as overtaking on double lines, using mobile phones while driving, failing to obey red traffic lights, not wearing seatbelts and queue-cutting, followed by vehicle licensing offences (686), other offences (562), technical offences (406), driver licensing offences (317) and goods-related offences (36). [[nid:1452980]] "A total of 73 vehicles were seized for various offences, with 71 seized under the Road Transport Act 1987 and two under the Land Public Transport Act 2010.

items.title: Sabah Watergate: Prosecution appeals 'inadequate' eight-year sentence
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The prosecution has filed an appeal against the sentence imposed on former Sabah Water Department director Ag Mohd Tahir Mohd Talib, as well as the discharge and acquittal of two co-accused in the high-profile Op Water case, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) said. The appeal, filed on May 25, challenges what the prosecution described as an "inadequate" eight-year sentence handed to Ag Mohd Tahir, along with the discharge and acquittal of his wife Fauziah Piut and former Sabah Water Department deputy director Lim Lam Beng. The court also found both Ag Mohd Tahir and Fauziah not guilty on two joint charges involving assets valued at approximately RM6.5 million.

items.title: Fahmi: Malaysians must heed King’s call to uphold Rukun Negara, preserve unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Politicians and Malaysians in general should heed and uphold the call by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for the people to embrace and practise the spirit and values of the Rukun Negara in their daily lives, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. “I respectfully uphold His Majesty’s call, and I believe politicians across the country will do the same. Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, graced the ceremony.

items.title: Social media platforms must comply with Malaysian laws - Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Social media platforms operating in Malaysia must respect and comply with the country's laws, particularly those relating to online safety and the protection of children, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the reminder comes as the government begins enforcing age-verification requirements for social media account registration from today through the implementation of the Child Protection Code (CPC) and Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). Fahmi said the two codes, introduced under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), represent a crucial first step in protecting individuals under the age of 16 from online harm.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolution speculation mounts as Onn Hafiz set to hold 5pm press conference
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — All eyes are on Johor today amid speculation that the state assembly could be dissolved. According to the New Straits Times , Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is scheduled to hold a special press conference at his official residence at 5pm. The announcement, eagerly awaited across the political divide, is expected to pave the way for state polls.

items.title: Fahmi: Social media platforms must respect Malaysian laws as age‑verification begins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Social media platforms operating in Malaysia must respect and comply with the country’s laws, particularly those relating to online safety and the protection of children, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the reminder comes as the government begins enforcing age-verification requirements for social media account registration from today through the implementation of the Child Protection Code (CPC) and Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). Fahmi said the two codes, introduced under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), represent a crucial first step in protecting individuals under the age of 16 from online harm.

items.title: Taiwan opposition leader 'very willing' to meet Trump on US trip
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun said on Monday that she would be "very willing" to meet US President Donald Trump when she visits the US this month. Cheng, chairwoman of the Kuomintang (KMT), Taiwan's largest opposition party, leaves for a two week US trip later on Monday, following on from her April visit to China where she met President Xi Jinping. Speaking to reporters in Taipei, Cheng said since the severing of diplomatic relations between Taipei and Washington in 1979, no Taiwanese leader had met a sitting US president, although she said her only official position was KMT chairwoman.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues alert for thunderstorms, heavy rain in seven states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit seven states until 5pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at 3.15 pm, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Perak are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, and Kuala Kangsar. In Selangor, the warning covers Hulu Langat and Sepang, while in Negeri Sembilan it involves Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau.

items.title: BN at 52: Zahid Hamidi rallies coalition for unity, renewal and resilience ahead polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called for mature politics, responsible leadership and practical solutions to address current challenges in an increasingly complex global environment. In conjunction with BN’s 52nd anniversary, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said the coalition must continue to move forward with a spirit of renewal, grounded in experience and an openness to adapt to future demands. Ahmad Zahid, who is Umno president, said BN was formed 52 years ago out of a spirit of understanding, moderation and cooperation among Malaysia’s diverse communities, to build the nation collectively.

items.title: Johor assembly to dissolve today? MB to hold special presser at 5pm
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Speculation is mounting that the Johor state assembly could be dissolved today, with Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi scheduled to hold a special press conference at his official residence at 5pm. STORY UPDATE: The Johor assembly has been dissolved . Onn Hafiz announced around 5.10pm that Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia and the Johor Regent, had consented to the dissolution of the assembly.

items.title: Kelantan JPJ: BMW 320i confiscated over fake road tax, five speeding summonses uncovered
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 1 — The Kelantan Road Transport Department (JPJ) seized a luxury BMW 320i on suspicion of displaying a fake road tax document during a special motorcycle operation held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidiladha Operations (HRAA) yesterday. Kelantan JPJ director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said that checks using the Smart Enforcement Device (SmED) found that the vehicle’s road tax had actually expired in July 2025, while the fake copy indicated that it was valid until August 2026. “The vehicle was seized under Section 108 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for allegedly displaying a forged document,” he told reporters after the HRAA 2026 Special Motorcycle Operation at the Malaysian Road Transport Academy (Eastern Region) here.

items.title: Obama's White House Instagram page hacked
items.summary: HOUSTON: The official White House Instagram page associated with former US President Barack Obama was hacked, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing a Monday report by entertainment news outlet TMZ. The hacker also reportedly posted new Instagram stories on the account, which has 2.4 million followers. Obama maintains a separate personal Instagram account under the handle @barackobama, which has 42.2 million followers.

items.title: Newlywed couple killed in Mersing crash days before moving into first home
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: A newlywed couple killed in a crash at Km19 of Jalan Endau-Mersing in Mersing yesterday had been looking forward to moving into their new home after returning from their Aidiladha celebration in Kelantan. Muhamad Bustaman Abdullah, 50, father of the late Muhammad Firdaus, 24, said he had recently bought a house in Taman Rinting here for his son and daughter-in-law, the late Siti Nur Aisyah Anoor Beet Babjan Kahar, 23. "I had already collected the keys to the new house before they left for Kelantan a day before Aidiladha.

items.title: Fahmi: Politicians, public must heed King's call, embrace Rukun Negara as guiding principle
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Politicians and Malaysians in general should heed and uphold the call by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for the people to embrace and practise the spirit and values of the Rukun Negara in their daily lives, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. "I respectfully uphold His Majesty's call, and I believe politicians across the country will do the same. Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, graced the ceremony.

items.title: Firefighters spend 20 hours rescuing 110kg hiker after fall at waterfall
items.summary: HULU LANGAT: Firefighters faced a gruelling 20-hour operation to rescue a 32-year-old man who broke his leg after falling during a hike at the Jeram Perlus Waterfall yesterday. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they were alerted at 12.58pm yesterday and arrived at the location at 1.31pm. A total of 10 personnel from the Bandar Tun Hussein Onn and Ampang Fire and Rescue stations were despatched.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain in 7 states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit seven states until 5pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at 3.15pm, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Perak are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, and Kuala Kangsar. In Selangor, the warning covers Hulu Langat and Sepang, while in Negri Sembilan it involves Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau.

items.title: Lack of trust, contradictory US positions, Israeli attacks on Lebanon delaying diplomacy: Iran
items.summary: DUBAI: A delay in the diplomatic process to end the US-Iran war can be explained by a lack of trust, Washington's contradictory positions and Israel's attacks on Lebanon, Iran's Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said on Monday. "Negotiations have started amid severe suspicion and mistrust, and the exchange of messages is taking place in this atmosphere," Baghaei said. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Four killed in five-vehicle crash near Simpang Renggam Prison
items.summary: KLUANG: Four people, including a 10-year-old child, were killed in a five-vehicle crash near Simpang Renggam Prison here today. The crash, which occurred at 1.16pm, involved a Perodua Alza, Proton Wira, Mercedes-Benz E240, BMW and Toyota Vios. Fire and Rescue Department operation commander Ismail Mamat confirmed the incident.

items.title: GOF joins helicopter‑deployed teams in Gunung Batu Putih search for Jaslinda Saludin missing May 23
items.summary: He said the GOF personnel, together with eight members of JBPM’s Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM), eight Forestry Department personnel and mountain guides, were flown into the search area aboard an MI-17 helicopter, significantly reducing travel time compared with previous overland routes through Gunung Batu Bujang and Pos Kuala Woh. Meanwhile, Perak JBPM director Datuk Sayani Saidon said SAR efforts remain focused on the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu, involving personnel from JBPM, the Forestry Department, mountain guides and the GOF. He said SAR operations were continuing in the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu involving personnel from JBPM, the Forestry Department, Forestry Mountain Guides and the GOF.

items.title: Nine‑year‑old boy dies after fall from 13th‑floor apartment in Ampang, police probe negligence
items.summary: Ampang Jaya police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said they received information regarding the incident from a woman at about 7.45pm after she found the victim unconscious. He said the boy was confirmed dead at the scene by an assistant medical officer from the Ampang Hospital. Following the incident, Khairul Anuar said police have recorded statements from five individuals, comprising the boy’s parents and family members as well as witnesses.

items.title: China enters flood season, heavy rain expected in the south
items.summary: HONG KONG: China has officially entered its flood season, with the south of the country expected to see heavy rainfall in June, the official Xinhua news agency reported, citing the Ministry of Water Resources. Rainfall in the northern part of north China, south China, and parts of southwest China is expected to be 10 to 20 per cent above average, Xinhua said. However, southern parts of north China, parts of northwest China, and the basins of the Yellow, Yangtze, and Huai rivers will see 10 to 20 per cent less rainfall than average.

items.title: Narathiwat council denies involvement in 'marry now, pay later' scheme targeting Malaysians
items.summary: NARATHIWAT: The Narathiwat Islamic Religious Council has denied involvement in a controversial "marry now, pay later" scheme allegedly targeting Malaysian couples. Its deputy president, Abdul Aziz Che Mamat, said all 28 members of the council's marriage administration committee had been cleared following an internal investigation into the matter. He said he believed the scheme, which recently sparked outrage among Malaysian netizens, was likely being offered by unauthorised individuals or groups operating outside the council's jurisdiction.

items.title: Two decades later, impacts from Indonesia mud volcano linger
items.summary: HARWATI clasped her hands and offered a prayer on the edge of a sludge lake on the Indonesian island of Java, as dozen of residents gathered to mark two decades since mud volcano eruptions began displacing thousands. Thirteen people died when an underground gas pipeline in the disaster zone exploded. Harwati said the gathering, held on Friday to mark two decades since the disaster, was organised to remind the government that residents are still feeling the impact of the mudflow.

items.title: Menteris besar, chief ministers extend congratulations on King's official birthday
items.summary: Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, in a social media post, prayed that His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, as well as the entire royal family, would be blessed with good health and the grace of Allah. Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun prayed that Malaysia would continue to enjoy peace, prosperity and well-being under His Majesty's reign, and be granted continued progress for the benefit of all Malaysians. Meanwhile, Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, on behalf of the state government, prayed that His Majesty's reign would continue to bring lasting blessings in steering the nation.

items.title: Perak mulls stricter hiking rules after recent cases
items.summary: TAIPING: The Perak state government will review and consider tightening standard operating procedures (SOPs) for hiking and forest-entry activities in the state to enhance visitor safety. State Science, Environment and Green Technology Committee chairman Teh Kok Lim said stricter measures could be introduced if deemed necessary following a review of the effectiveness of existing SOPs. He said the current SOPs were generally adequate, but their effectiveness depended largely on compliance and cooperation from all parties, particularly hikers.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi demands DAP disclosure of GLC posts as Marina Ibrahim’s retirement sparks political uproar
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi has accused DAP of using government‑linked company (GLC) positions as political bargaining chips, following controversy over a purported offer involving Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim. Puad claimed the matter came to light when Marina allegedly rejected a proposal to contest the Tiram state seat in exchange for a GLC post. “DAP is worse.

items.title: Airlifted GOF team boosts search efforts for missing hiker Jaslinda [WATCH]
items.summary: He said the GOF personnel, together with eight members of the department's Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM), eight Forestry Department personnel and mountain guides, were flown into the search area aboard an MI-17 helicopter, significantly reducing travel time compared with previous overland routes through Gunung Batu Bujang and Pos Kuala Woh. Meanwhile, Perak fire department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said SAR efforts remain focused on the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu, involving personnel from the department, the Forestry Department, mountain guides and the GOF. He said SAR operations were continuing in the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu involving personnel from the fire department, the Forestry Department, Forestry Mountain Guides and the GOF.

items.title: Well Chip's growth outlook strengthened by new outlets, says Maybank IB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Well Chip Group Bhd's growth prospects remain firmly supported by its outlet expansion and regional diversification efforts, according to Maybank Investment Bank Bhd (Maybank IB). The expansion comes as the pawnbroker and gold retailer posted stronger-than-expected first quarter earnings, with net profit surging 56 per cent year-on-year to RM27.9 million. "Moving forward, prospects for Well Chip remain supported by its ongoing outlet expansion and regional diversification efforts," the firm said in a note.

items.title: Netanyahu ordered military to attack targets in Beirut's southern suburbs
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday ordered the military to attack targets in the Lebanese capital Beirut's southern suburbs, a Hizbollah stronghold known as Dahiyeh. "Following repeated violations of the ceasefire in Lebanon by the terrorist group Hizbollah and the attacks against our cities and citizens, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Israel Katz ordered the IDF to attack terrorist targets in the Dahiyeh district in Beirut," a statement from Netanyahu's office said. Israeli troops and Hizbollah have continued to trade fire since a mid-April ceasefire, with Hizbollah resorting to the use of cheap, easy-to-assemble kamikaze drones that are hard for air defences to thwart and that have killed several Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Skudai DAP rep Marina Ibrahim hits back at ‘bought over’ rumours amid purported GLC offer
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim today rejected claims she was defecting or had been “bought over”, saying her political decisions were made after consulting her closest supporters and stressing her focus was on solving problems rather than constant confrontation. In a lengthy Facebook post, Marina invoked Sun Tzu’s Art of War to defend her non-confrontational style, arguing that “the best victory is one achieved without fighting”. In a Berita Harian report, Marina said she had shared “the letter” in a Johor DAP WhatsApp group.

items.title: Taxpayers can now file assessment appeals online through MyTax and track status in real time, says IRB
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 1 — Taxpayers can now file assessment appeals or apply for an extension of time to appeal tax assessments raised under the Income Tax Act 1967 through the new e-Assessment Appeal service on the MyTax portal. “Among the new experiences for taxpayers include the ability to submit assessment appeals or applications for extension of time from anywhere and in real time,” the IRB said. For access, the IRB said individual taxpayers may submit appeals or extension applications via the individual taxpayer role on MyTax.

items.title: Melaka police: Viral video edited, no escort of foreign nationals in Tanjung Minyak
items.summary: MELAKA, June 1 — Police have denied claims that they were escorting several vehicles allegedly carrying foreign nationals in Tanjung Minyak here. Acting Melaka Tengah police chief Supt Halim Abas said the viral video in question was believed to have been edited by combining footage from two different locations. He said checks found that the footage showing a police four-wheel-drive vehicle exiting a junction was indeed recorded near Masjid Tun Mohd Khalil Yaakub in Tanjung Minyak.

items.title: IT consultant loses nearly RM1.5m after installing fake ‘Kakaue’ crypto investment app
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 1 — An IT consultant has lost nearly RM1.5 million after falling victim to an online cryptocurrency investment scam syndicate known as ‘Crypto Kakaue’. Azizee said the victim subsequently joined a WhatsApp group chat called ‘Saham Mececah Hati Orang’, where he was told about the investment and offered high returns. He said the victim was told to install an app called ‘Kakaue’ and conduct cryptocurrency transactions.

items.title: Police arrest two women after Myanmar man found stabbed to death in Simpang Ampat house
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, June 1 — Police have detained two women to assist in investigations into the murder of a Myanmar national at a house in Taman Belibis, Simpang Ampat here yesterday, bringing the total number of suspects arrested to three. Seberang Perai Selatan (SPS) district police chief Supt Jay January Siowou said the arrests followed the detention of the main suspect, a 38-year-old man who was apprehended by members of the public at the scene before being handed over to the police yesterday. He said preliminary investigations found that the incident is believed to have stemmed from a domestic dispute.

items.title: On RMAF’s 68th anniversary, defence minister says Mindef pledges to boost force’s capabilities and readiness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) remains committed to strengthening the capabilities and readiness of the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) through the development of assets, technology and human capital to ensure the force remains at the forefront of defending the country’s airspace. The history of the RMAF began when the Air Force Ordinance was passed by Parliament on June 2, 1958, with the force initially known as the Royal Federal Air Force. Following the formation of Malaysia on Sept 16, 1963, the force was subsequently renamed the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF).

items.title: Sabah detains 300 in Likas raid against deviant religious group banned by state fatwa council
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 1 — Some 300 individuals, including 12 key leaders and organisers of a deviant religious group, were detained in a joint enforcement operation conducted by the Sabah Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAINS) and the police in Likas here today. Acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said the operation was carried out following a public complaint received by JHEAINS on May 28 regarding the ‘Jalsa Salana Wilayah Sabah Ke-32’ programme held from May 29 to 31. He said checks found that the programme was linked to the Ahmadiyah Qadiani movement, which was declared deviant and contrary to Islamic teachings by the Sabah State Fatwa Council and gazetted in 2007.

items.title: King confers posthumous award on Survey and Mapping Dept officer who died on duty at Malaysia–Brunei border
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — A Malaysian Survey and Mapping Department (JUPEM) personnel, Donysius Gabriel Goh, who died while on duty at the Malaysia–Brunei border in March this year, has been posthumously awarded the Bintang Jasa Perkasa Persekutuan (JPP) in recognition of his service and contributions. According to the official list of recipients for the 2026 federal awards, medals and honours in conjunction with the Official Birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, the late Donysius is the sole recipient of the honour. A total of 161 individuals were conferred federal honours in conjunction with this year’s official birthday celebration of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia.

items.title: Ministers extend congratulations, prayers to King on official birthday
items.summary: Meanwhile, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said prayed that His Majesty would be blessed with divine grace to continue safeguarding the people with wisdom, dignity and compassion for the prosperity and well-being of the nation. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, likewise, extended his highest congratulations and heartfelt loyalty to His Majesty in conjunction with the historic occasion. Congratulatory messages were also conveyed by Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek; Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin; and Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali.

items.title: DPMs Fadillah, Zahid convey congratulations to King on official birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof has extended his congratulations to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, on the occasion of the King’s Official Birthday. “May the King continue to reign with majesty, sovereignty and honour, guiding the nation and its people with wisdom, fairness and prudence,” according to the post. Meanwhile, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi also conveyed his highest congratulations and royal greetings to the King in conjunction with His Majesty’s Official Birthday.

items.title: Johor Regent, MB lead Bangsa Johor in extending birthday greetings to King
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — The Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, today conveyed his greetings and prayers to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, in conjunction with His Majesty’s official birthday celebration. “Allah Peliharakan Sultan. “I humbly extend my highest greetings and congratulations to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, in conjunction with His Majesty’s official birthday celebration today.

items.title: Anwar praises King as symbol of unity, vows Madani govt will continue serving with dedication
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today conveyed his highest appreciation to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for his patronage and wisdom in guiding the nation under the principles of Constitutional Monarchy and Parliamentary Democracy. Anwar said under His Majesty’s patronage, the Madani Government continues to move forward with dedication and confidence in carrying out its responsibilities to govern the country. He said the government remained strong through the loyalty and unity of Malaysians, who continue to uphold the spirit and determination to build the nation despite various challenges, including racial divisions.

items.title: King: Politicians must watch their words, avoid misuse of free speech
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today ordered all political leaders to uphold proper conduct and decorum, and to exercise caution when making public statements. Sultan Ibrahim said politicians should refrain from provoking sensitive issues, especially ahead of election periods, which often become a platform for individuals to seek prominence and win public support. “‘Jangan kerana nila setitik, rosak susu sebelanga’ (do not let one bad deed spoil the whole endeavour),” Sultan Ibrahim said in his royal address at the investiture ceremony for Federal awards, honours and medals, held in conjunction with the King’s official birthday celebration at Istana Negara today.

items.title: Those who reject Rukun Negara principles unfit to be called Malaysians, says King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today said that any individual who rejects the principles of the Rukun Negara is not worthy of being regarded as a Malaysian citizen. His Majesty said the Rukun Negara, introduced on Aug 31, 1970, is a national pledge and commitment by all Malaysians to restore unity and strengthen social harmony among the country’s diverse communities. “Any Malaysian who refuses to adhere to the principles of the Rukun Negara, I advise, the person is not worthy of being called a Malaysian citizen,” said Sultan Ibrahim.

items.title: Police probe murder after Myanmar man killed, wife injured in Simpang Ampat attack
items.summary: Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said a 38-year-old Myanmar man was arrested following the incident to assist in investigations. In the attack, the 31-year-old male victim died at the scene, while a 26-year-old woman, believed to be his wife and also a Myanmar national, sustained injuries and was taken to Hospital Seberang Jaya (HSJ) for treatment, he said. “The victim died as a result of the incident, while the injured woman was sent to Hospital Seberang Jaya for further medical treatment,” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Chief Justice Wan Ahmad Farid heads King’s 2026 birthday honours list with ‘Tun’ award
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh headed the list of 144 recipients of Federal Awards, Medals, and Honours conferred in conjunction with the Official Birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, at Istana Negara today. Meanwhile, Treasury Secretary-General Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican, Foreign Ministry Secretary-General Datuk Seri Amran Mohamed Zin, and Army Chief Gen Datuk Azhan Md Othman were among five individuals conferred the Panglima Setia Mahkota (PSM) award, which also carries the title ‘Tan Sri’. Air Force Chief Gen Datuk Seri Muhamad Norazlan Aris and Yayasan Sultan Ibrahim Johor trustee Datuk Avinderjit Singh Harjit Singh were also awarded the PSM.
